rationale_for_baseline_tier: A 3-week-old with a sustained (20-minute) tetanic episode of carpopedal and facial spasm that does not settle with restraint, transient perioral cyanosis with breath-holding, poor feeding, and an unrepaired conotruncal heart defect. Untreated symptomatic neonatal hypocalcaemia progresses rapidly to generalised seizures, laryngospasm and fatal arrhythmia, and the airway/cyanosis feature signals an immediate threat. This is a life-threatening neonatal emergency — T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier. The definitive treatment (IV calcium with cardiac monitoring) is hospital-only; the telehealth service recognises and escalates.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sustained spasm/twitching in the neonate that does not settle, especially not abolished by gently holding the limb
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 now, do not wait for the episode to pass
escalation_edges.rationale: Pathological tetany/seizure in a neonate is an emergency until a treatable cause is excluded in hospital; delay risks progression to seizures and arrhythmia.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This is an emergency for such a young baby. Call 000 for an ambulance right now — tell them he's 3 weeks old, has a known heart condition, and is having sustained twitching and spasms that won't stop.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Blue lips/face, breath-holding, pauses in breathing, floppiness or unresponsiveness
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — stay on the line with 000
escalation_edges.rationale: Cyanosis/apnoea signals airway compromise (laryngospasm) or arrhythmia — an immediate life threat requiring paramedic resuscitation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his lips or face go blue, he stops breathing, or he becomes floppy or unresponsive, keep him on his back with his head neutral, stay on the line with 000, and follow the call-taker's resuscitation instructions.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Future episodes of facial twitching, hand cramping or jitteriness after discharge
escalation_edges.rationale: Recurrent symptomatic hypocalcaemia remains life-threatening; carers are instructed to call 000 for any recurrence and never to feed during a spasm.
escalation_edges.communication_template: In future, if he develops facial twitching, hand cramping or jitteriness again, call 000 straight away and do not feed him during the episode.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: 3-week-old neonate — non-verbal, fully dependent, medically fragile (unrepaired conotruncal heart defect, cleft palate)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: The lowest threshold for escalation applies: a neonate cannot self-report, deteriorates fast, and the underlying cardiac lesion compounds arrhythmia risk. No home management, no medication dosing — recognise and call 000.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Overwhelmed first-time parents managing complex cardiac medication charts
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear, single-step instructions (call 000, neutral head position, do not feed during a spasm, gather the medicine charts); arrange social-work/care-coordination support after the emergency.
Heightens choking/aspiration risk — reinforces the instruction not to feed during a spasm.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance immediately — this is an emergency for a 3-week-old. While you wait, lay him on his back with his head in a neutral position, keep his airway clear, and do NOT try to feed him during the twitching or spasms because of the choking risk. Gather his cardiac medicines and charts for the paramedics and unlock the door for them.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this must not wait for clinic hours or an in-hours appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary with the calcium results, cardiac monitoring findings and any genetic testing; take these to the GP, the cardiac team and the paediatrician for ongoing calcium/vitamin D management.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a sustained neonatal tetanic/seizure-like episode with cyanosis as benign jitteriness or a routine problem, or attributing it to the known disability and advising home observation.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Untreated hypocalcaemia progresses to generalised seizures, laryngospasm and fatal cardiac arrhythmia within hours.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising self-transport to an emergency department rather than an emergency ambulance for a neonate with airway-threat features — deterioration (apnoea/arrhythmia) may occur en route without paramedic monitoring.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: For this case T5 is correct, not over-triage; over-triage would only apply to a genuinely benign, self-settling jitteriness with no cyanosis and prompt settling on restraint — a low-harm error vastly preferable to under-triage here.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot distinguish hypocalcaemic tetany from a neonatal seizure, sepsis or hypoglycaemia, nor measure calcium, glucose or the ECG.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Do not attempt to resolve the cause remotely — escalate the acutely unwell neonate to emergency care (000) where calcium, glucose, ECG and a sepsis screen can be done and IV calcium given.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Trying to differentiate the cause over chat, advising home observation/feeding, or reassuring the parents that it is normal newborn jitteriness.
